articles.title: The use of artificial intelligence in the diagnosis of odontogenic cysts and tumors
articles.authors: Souza LL; Roza ALOC; Giraldo-Roldán D; Correia-Neto IJ; Lopes MA; Khurram SA; et al.
articles.keywords: artificial intelligence; odontogenic cysts; odontogenic tumors; CT; histopathology; CBCT
articles.abstract: This literature review examines the latest advancements and challenges in using AI for diagnosing odontogenic cysts and tumors. These lesions present overlapping clinical and radiographic features complicating accurate diagnosis. AI, particularly ML and DL models, offers solutions by enhancing automation and precision. Numerous studies highlight AI algorithms analyzing radiographs, CT, and histopathological slides, achieving diagnostic outcomes comparable to expert clinicians. Key challenges remain: limited datasets, class imbalance, interpretability, and lack of external validation.
